mission.title: k8sgpt: [Bug]: k8sgpt fails to start with "mkdir /.config: permission denied"
mission.description: [Bug]: k8sgpt fails to start with "mkdir /.config: permission denied". Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify k8sgpt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your k8sgpt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8sgpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8sgpt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8sgpt -n k8sgpt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review k8sgpt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k8sgpt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n k8sgpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8sgpt
kubectl get configmap -n k8sgpt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=k8sgpt
```
- [X] I've searched for similar issues and couldn't find anything matching
- [X] I've included steps to reproduce the behavior

### Affected Components

- [X] #470

### K8sGPT Version

v0.3.0

### Kubernetes Version

v1.24.0

### Host OS and its

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: k8sgpt fails to start with "mkdir /.config:…
steps.description:
## 📑 Description
with this PR k8sgpt should also be able to run with an arbitrary uid. that is necessary especially in openshift environments because there by default the restricted-v2 SCC sets a uid in the securityContext. currently it depends on uid 65532 because k8sgpt wants to create a .config
```yaml
kubectl logs k8sgpt-deployment-877c6ddd9-2cx5z
Error: mkdir /.config: permission denied
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: k8sgpt fails to start with "mkdir… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8sgpt -n k8sgpt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n k8sgpt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## 📑 Description
with this PR k8sgpt should also be able to run with an arbitrary uid. that is necessary especially in openshift environments because there by default the restricted-v2 SCC sets a uid in the securityContext.
codeSnippets:
kubectl logs k8sgpt-deployment-877c6ddd9-2cx5z
Error: mkdir /.config: permission denied

securityContext:
    fsGroup: 1000980000
    seLinuxOptions:
      level: s0:c31,c25
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 1000980000

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with k8sgpt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
